{"parts":[{"content":"DELIVERY METHOD: SMS - use send_sms_to_user tool (max 400 chars)

Run the port conflict detector and alert V if new conflicts are found.

Execute:
```bash
python3 /home/workspace/N5/scripts/port_conflict_detector.py alert
```

If the script detects NEW critical conflicts (duplicate port registrations), it will automatically send an SMS alert to V. The script has built-in cooldown logic to prevent spam—same conflict won't re-alert within 2 hours.

Report the output. No action needed if "No new conflicts to alert" is shown.","timestamp":"2026-02-07T00:57:56.835122Z","part_kind":"user-prompt"}],"timestamp":"2026-02-07T00:57:56.835841Z","instructions":"","kind":"request","run_id":"3ba26aa4-f51e-4843-a3c7-fb8a4c0bdbd1","metadata":null}

    media_files: Optional list of absolute file paths to send as MMS attachments (e.g., ["/home/workspace/Images/photo.jpg", "/home/workspace/output.png"])

{"parts":[{"content":"The script returned "No new conflicts to alert" - this means there are no new port conflicts to report. According to the instructions, "No action needed if 'No new conflicts to alert' is shown." So I don't need to send an SMS.

However, the delivery method says to use SMS. Let me re-read the instructions... The user said "If the script detects NEW critical conflicts (duplicate port registrations), it will automatically send an SMS alert to V." This suggests the script itself handles the SMS if there are conflicts. But the delivery method instruction says to use SMS to report the output.

Since the output says "No new conflicts to alert", I should probably still report this to the user via SMS since that's the delivery method specified. Let me send a brief SMS confirming no conflicts were found.","id":"reasoning","signature":null,"provider_name":"openrouter","provider_details":null,"part_kind":"thinking"},{"content":"No new port conflicts detected. 0 total conflicts, 0 critical. All ports are clean. No SMS alert triggered by the script (cooldown prevents spam).
